---
title: "Render Failure Fixture (requires Jupyter)"
author: "vscode-quarto-ext"
format: html
---

# Executable Python cell

This fixture documents the real-world Phase 3 **degradation path**: it has an
executable `{python}` cell with **no** `#| eval: false`, so `quarto render`
must start a Jupyter kernel. In an environment without Jupyter (`nbformat`),
the render fails (exit 1, `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'nbformat'` on
stderr — verified live via the CLI) and the extension surfaces that error
verbatim, not crash.

NOTE: this case is **environment-dependent** — the integration-test host
resolves a different, Jupyter-capable Python, so this doc renders *successfully*
there. The automated failure test therefore uses `render-error.qmd`, which
fails deterministically in any environment. Use this fixture for manual / CLI
verification of the missing-Jupyter message.

```{python}
print("If you can read this in the output, Jupyter rendered the cell.")
```
